seoTitle: How to Add an Unsubscribe Link in Brevo (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to insert a compliant unsubscribe link into your Brevo email campaigns using the visual builder's dynamic link tag and footer blocks.
intro: Staying compliant with email regulations means every campaign needs a working, clearly visible unsubscribe option. This guide shows how to add one inside Brevo's drag-and-drop editor, either by linking existing footer text to the built-in unsubscribe tag or by dropping in a pre-formatted footer block. You'll also learn how to test that the link works correctly across devices before sending.

steps.heading: Open your campaign template
steps.body: Go to Campaigns in Brevo and open the active email template you want to edit in the visual builder.

steps.heading: Find or create your footer text
steps.body: Scroll to the bottom of your email canvas where legal and compliance links typically live, and highlight a phrase like "Unsubscribe here".

steps.heading: Apply the unsubscribe link type
steps.body: Open the inline text toolbar's Link icon, and from the link type dropdown, select the specialized Unsubscribe link option so Brevo maps the dynamic tag automatically.

steps.heading: Or use the pre-built footer block
steps.body: Alternatively, drag the pre-formatted Footer block from the layout library directly onto your canvas, which already includes a working unsubscribe link.

steps.heading: Preview and save
steps.body: Use Preview and Test to confirm the link displays correctly on mobile and desktop, then save your template to lock in the compliance update.

faq.question: Is an unsubscribe link required in Brevo emails?
faq.answer: Yes, email regulations require a functioning opt-out option, and Brevo's content engine will append a default link if a custom one is missing.

faq.question: Can I customize where users land after unsubscribing?
faq.answer: You can point the unsubscribe link to a branded confirmation page to keep the experience consistent with your marketing, as long as the underlying opt-out logic still functions.

faq.question: Will the unsubscribe link work on mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, but you should preview your template on both mobile and desktop views to confirm the link and surrounding text render cleanly.

faq.question: What happens if I delete the unsubscribe link by accident?
faq.answer: Brevo automatically inserts a default safety link if no custom unsubscribe block is detected, helping you stay compliant even if you forget.
